headline: NEM Promised Needs-Based Policy in 2010 — Race-Based Criteria Persist
context: The New Economic Model launched in 2010 promised a shift from race-based to needs-based economic policy. In 2026, 94% of federal assistance programmes still use racial eligibility criteria.

cards.big: The NEM promised needs-based policy 16 years ago.
cards.sub: 94% of federal assistance programmes still require applicants to identify their race. Needs-based remains a slogan.

cards.big: B40 Bumiputera receive 8.2 times more per capita assistance than B40 non-Bumiputera.
cards.sub: Both groups earn below RM3,170. The assistance gap is racial, not economic.

cards.big: The NEM's own 2015 midpoint review recommended 'accelerated transition.'
cards.sub: No acceleration occurred. The 2020 review was not published.

cards.big: Brazil transitioned from race-based to income-based quotas in university admissions in 2012.
cards.sub: Minority access increased 4% while public support rose from 48% to 73%.

cards.big: Promising needs-based policy while maintaining race-based programmes is not transition. It is two narratives for two audiences.
Needs-based policy that accounts for historical disadvantage can achieve equity objectives without racial criteria. The tools exist. The political will to use them does not.
